content: CRITICAL: JWT has NO expiration — createTerminalJWT in lib/terminal/jwt.ts only calls setIssuedAt(), no setExpirationTime. Tokens are effectively immortal until secret rotation.

CRITICAL: /api/device/token/refresh sets clockTolerance: 999_999_999 — even if exp existed it would be ignored. Refresh issues new token from any signed token regardless of expiry — no replay/rotation protection.
CRITICAL: app/api/payment/cancel/route.ts uses body.termId instead of auth.payload.termId — spoofable termId in cancel-request → process cancellations on other merchant terminals.
HIGH: terminals/[id]/account PUT does not verify merchant ownership of the terminal id — any authenticated user can set terminal_account_id and password for ANY terminal (account takeover).
HIGH: terminals/[id]/key PUT does not check ownership either — same takeover risk for merchant_key linkage. Could redirect payment keys.

HIGH: device/activate stores accessToken in terminals.access_token DB column — long-lived bearer token at rest. If DB read leaked, all terminals compromised. activation_code is only 8 hex chars (32 bits) — bruteforceable; rate limit 5/15min/IP bypassed by multiple IPs.

HIGH: bcryptjs PIN with rounds=10 acceptable, but PIN default 1234 in terminals POST + adminPin default 1234. 4-digit PIN space=10000 → bcrypt round=10 ~100ms = ~1000s offline crack. Critical if hash leaked.

MEDIUM: rate limit is in-memory Map — on serverless this is per-instance, attacker bypasses by spreading requests across cold starts/instances. Also keyed by x-forwarded-for first hop (spoofable if header trusted).
MEDIUM: No CSRF protection for /api/auth/signout and cookie-auth routes; POST with credentials from any origin could trigger sign-out / settings changes. No SameSite/Origin checks visible.
MEDIUM: device/auth returns 401 for both unknown user and bad password (good — no user enum). But ACCOUNT_NOT_CONFIGURED reveals account exists without hash (info leak).

LOW: TERMINAL_JWT_SECRET fallback uses ! non-null assertion; if env missing in dev, jose throws at runtime — acceptable but no startup validation. HS256 symmetric — service must keep secret away from clients.
